methods.description: Process for preparing sedimentary rock samples for pollen grain investigation using light microscopy (LM) and scanning electron microscopy (SEM).

steps.description: Sedimentary rock samples were processed following the protocol described in Grímsson et al. (2008).
steps.searchable_summary: process sedimentary rock samples according to Grímsson et al. (2008) protocol

steps.description: The same pollen grains were investigated using LM and SEM (single grain method, Zetter 1989).
steps.searchable_summary: investigate pollen grains using LM and SEM

steps.description: LM photographs of dispersed fossil pollen were taken with an Olympus BX51.
steps.searchable_summary: capture LM photographs of fossil pollen using Olympus BX51

steps.description: Specimens were sputter coated with gold and photographed using a Hitachi S-4300 cold field emission scanning electron microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: sputter coat specimens with gold and photograph using Hitachi S-4300 SEM

steps.description: For the pollen diagram, 400 palynomorphs were counted per sample.
steps.searchable_summary: count 400 palynomorphs per sample for pollen diagram

steps.description: The pollen diagram (Fig. 2) was generated in C2 vers. 1.7.6.
steps.searchable_summary: generate pollen diagram using C2 version 1.7.6

steps.description: Maps and sections were drawn in Adobe Illustrator 15.0.0 and photographs were cropped in Adobe Photoshop 12.0.
steps.searchable_summary: draw maps and sections in Adobe Illustrator and crop photos in Adobe Photoshop
